intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Puliculum articulatum." However, based on a possible match, I will assume that the plant you are referring to is Pulicaria articulata, also known as Pulicaria dysenterica. If that is the case, here's a paragraph about it:

Pulicaria articulata, also known as Pulicaria dysenterica, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pulicaria articulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pulicaria articulata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and it is also used in some cultures as a treatment for dysentery and other gastrointestinal issues.
